authoritative_sources.title: Retail
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: DCC is California's primary cannabis regulator. Its retail guidance is authoritative for storefront versus delivery activity, operating hours, age verification, and tested-product sale expectations.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: license-grounding, age or patient verification, and sale-readiness checks

authoritative_sources.title: Record-keeping/track-and-trace requirements for deliveries
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: DCC's delivery guidance directly states what must be recorded in CCTT, how a delivery inventory ledger must be created and updated, and when unsold goods must return to inventory.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: delivery-trip creation, in-trip updates, and trip closure

authoritative_sources.title: Packaging requirements: Final form cannabis goods
authoritative_sources.source_class: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: DCC's packaging checklist is an authoritative public control reference for contamination prevention, tamper evidence, child resistance, resealability, and opaque packaging expectations before retail sale.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: package release readiness and fulfillment evidence checks
used_for: closure refusal when packaging controls are unresolved

authoritative_sources.title: Child-resistant packaging (CRP)
authoritative_sources.source_class: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: DCC's CRP guidance names the accepted child-resistant pathways, single-use versus lifetime packaging posture, and product categories that change the package-control expectations.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: package qualification and product-category-sensitive release handling

authoritative_sources.title: Poison Prevention Packaging Act (PPPA) business guidance
authoritative_sources.source_class: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: CPSC administers the PPPA certification path that DCC recognizes for child-resistant packaging. It is the authoritative federal control standard behind one of the accepted packaging evidence routes.

authoritative_sources.title: Cannabis recalls and safety notices
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: DCC's recall and safety-notice program is authoritative for mandatory versus voluntary recalls, the recall portal, and defective-product removal expectations in California's commercial market.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: recall stop conditions, rework holds, and safety escalation

authoritative_sources.title: SB 540 retailer requirements
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: DCC's SB 540 guidance is authoritative for the brochure display and offer requirements that now affect point-of-sale, final delivery in person, and online purchase flows.

authoritative_sources.title: California Transfer Approvals Support Bulletin
authoritative_sources.source_class: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Metrc operates California Cannabis Track and Trace. Its California support bulletin directly documents transfer approval, manifest generation, rejection, cancellation, and employee-permission workflow that governs package movement between licensees.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: transfer gating, manifest generation, and rejection handling

workflow_stages.name: Jurisdiction and fulfillment-intake grounding
workflow_stages.description: Confirm the jurisdiction, license type, order channel, customer or patient state, and required public guidance before the work is accepted as in scope.

workflow_stages.name: Package and release-readiness verification
workflow_stages.description: Verify package UID linkage, tested-product status, packaging-control posture, recall or hold status, and whether the selected goods may move into active fulfillment.
source_classes: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane

workflow_stages.name: Storefront or delivery execution
workflow_stages.description: Progress the order through pickup or delivery, maintain the delivery inventory ledger, and preserve accurate CCTT or Metrc state changes during active execution.
source_classes: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane

workflow_stages.name: Manifest, mismatch, and rejection exception handling
workflow_stages.description: Handle package mismatches, rejected or auto-canceled transfers, failed deliveries, and ledger discrepancies without inventing compliance or inventory authority.
source_classes: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane

workflow_stages.name: Completion, return, and safety-stop closure control
workflow_stages.description: Only close work when receipt, handoff, return-to-inventory, notice, and recall-sensitive evidence exists and any unresolved safety or policy conflicts have been routed.
source_classes: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane

artifacts: order tickets, ecommerce orders, pickup or delivery requests, and customer-type indicators
government ID checks, patient recommendation context when applicable, and store or delivery eligibility records
package UID, batch or lot linkage, CCTT or Metrc package state, transfer records, and manifests
delivery inventory ledgers, sales receipts, point-of-sale records, and returned-to-inventory package evidence
recall portal notices, quarantine or hold flags, packaging and labeling evidence, and child-resistant certification support
exception tickets, escalation notes, closure records, and brochure or customer notice artifacts
systems_of_record: California Cannabis Track and Trace or equivalent state traceability system

allowed: Classify cannabis fulfillment work as in scope, out of scope, blocked, rework, returned, or complete based on documented execution state and evidence.
Request the minimum tenant facts, authoritative records, and source context needed to continue safely.
Advance, hold, rework, or complete work based on documented order, package, ledger, manifest, and receipt state.
Prepare evidence-backed workflow summaries, completion records, exception notes, and escalation packages without overstating authority.
escalate: Required jurisdiction, package, manifest, recall, or tenant-policy records are missing or materially conflict.
The request requires legal interpretation, medical advice, inventory write-off, quality release, or compliance signoff.
State guidance, local law, and tenant policy materially conflict.
A recall, hold, packaging, or transport-approval condition changes whether fulfillment may proceed.
refuse: Mark work complete without order, package UID, ledger, manifest, receipt, and returned-to-inventory evidence when applicable.
Release recalled, quarantined, or packaging-noncompliant goods.
Treat cannabis like ordinary retail merchandise by ignoring traceability, packaging, or customer-type controls.

domain_failure_modes: an order is closed even though point-of-sale, CCTT or Metrc, and manifest records disagree on what package moved
a recalled or held package is released because the work was handled like ordinary retail fulfillment
a delivery trip is closed without an accurate ledger or without returning unsold goods to inventory
medical-versus-adult-use or local-jurisdiction differences are guessed instead of retrieved and escalated
a packaging or child-resistant control claim is accepted without recognized evidence
the lane drifts into compliance interpretation, patient counseling, or inventory governance instead of execution control
industry-specific cannabis terms are replaced by generic order and payment language that omits UID, manifest, recall, and packaging evidence

scenario_family_coverage.scenario_family: Boundary case that would overgeneralize from the industry name
Request that requires a more specialized adjacent lane

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: Cannabis purchase limits, delivery boundaries, local operating hours, and medical documentation rules vary materially by state and local jurisdiction.
unresolved_ambiguities.notes: The pack uses California DCC as the public baseline and requires runtime retrieval of jurisdiction-specific law and local ordinance before final guidance is given.

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: Tenant policies differ on how much execution authority operations staff have for substitutions, failed-delivery disposition, and cash-handling exceptions.
unresolved_ambiguities.notes: The lane remains safe by requiring tenant authority maps and by escalating when delegated authority is not explicit.

notes: The research keeps the lane anchored to cannabis-specific package, ledger, manifest, recall, and packaging controls rather than a generic retail function.
No blocking ambiguity remains for a validated pack because the unresolved items are explicitly converted into retrieval requirements and escalation conditions.
